title: Aureline environment-capsule body and export-manifest record
description: Boundary schema for the environment-capsule body and its export-manifest projection. Every Aureline execution surface that claims a 'this is the environment I ran against' truth — terminal session seed, task run, test run, debug attach, refactor-preview probe, AI/apply sandbox, hosted review replay — reads and emits exactly this record shape. The execution-context record (schemas/runtime/execution_context.schema.json) references an environment capsule through environment_capsule_ref and quotes its capsule_id / capsule_hash / resolved_schema_version / drift_state / compatibility_fingerprint; this schema is the full body that reference resolves against. The capsule is content-addressed: capsule_hash MUST be the digest of the canonical capsule body bytes. Re-exports the frozen target-identity, toolchain-identity, filesystem-identity, capability-envelope, authority-envelope, compatibility-fingerprint, drift-state, redaction-class, and secret-class vocabularies from schemas/runtime/execution_context.schema.json and ADR-0007; this schema does not mint new authority, target, toolchain, or secret classes. Raw environment bodies, raw command lines, raw secret values, and raw filesystem paths outside the ADR-0006 filesystem-identity record never appear unless an explicit broadened_capture opt-in is recorded on the export manifest; credential_alias handles and secret-class labels cross the boundary, never raw bytes. Adding a new capsule_location_class, a new capsule_origin_class, a new secret_projection_mode, a new export_sink_class, or a new capsule audit event id is additive-minor and bumps environment_capsule_schema_version; repurposing an existing field is breaking and requires a new decision row.

environment_capsule_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this environment-capsule vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp captured from a monotonic clock source at capsule assembly or export.

opaque_id.description: Opaque stable identifier safe to log and export. Raw secrets, raw absolute paths outside the ADR-0006 filesystem-identity record, raw URLs, and raw content bodies never appear here.

short_sentence.description: Short reviewer-facing sentence safe to show in capsule inspectors, export reviews, and support bundles. Raw secret values, raw env bodies, and raw command lines never appear here.

dotted_lower_snake_id.description: Canonical dotted lower-snake id. Used for capsule ids, toolchain ids, activator ids, template ids, and prebuild ids.

filesystem_identity_record.description: Re-export of the ADR-0006 five-layer filesystem-identity record. Raw absolute paths appear only here, on presentation_path, where later export redaction can collapse them to canonical_filesystem_object / save_target_token before emission.

identity_mode.description: Re-export of the ADR-0001 identity-mode vocabulary.

trust_state.description: Re-export of the ADR-0001 workspace-trust state vocabulary.

policy_epoch.description: Policy epoch at capsule resolve time. Stale projections surface a visible epoch delta.

workspace_id.description: Stable workspace identifier copied from the workspace authority.

target_class.description: Re-export of the frozen target-class vocabulary from schemas/runtime/execution_context.schema.json.

reachability_state.description: Re-export of the frozen target-reachability vocabulary.

route_dependency_class.description: Re-export of the network-route class for remote targets; null for local targets.

capability_envelope_ref.description: Re-export of the execution-target capability envelope from the execution-context schema. The capsule body quotes the envelope by ref; it does not re-derive capability claims.

target_identity.description: Re-export of the frozen target-identity record from schemas/runtime/execution_context.schema.json. The capsule embeds target identity so support-bundle, replay, and build-target-discovery consumers read one shape.

toolchain_class.description: Re-export of the frozen toolchain-class vocabulary.

activation_strategy.description: Re-export of the frozen activation-strategy vocabulary.

unsupported_gap_record.description: Re-export of the frozen unsupported-gap record from the execution-context schema.

toolchain_identity.description: Re-export of the frozen toolchain-identity record. This field is the toolchain-identity field set the contract promises to support-bundle, execution-context, and build-target discovery artifacts.

capsule_drift_state.description: Re-export of the ADR-0006 drift-state vocabulary for a capsule's declarative inputs.

compatibility_fingerprint.description: Re-export of the frozen compatibility-fingerprint record. Prebuild-reuse and capsule-reuse mismatches downgrade the cache disposition rather than silently serving stale tools.

capsule_location_class.description: Where this capsule materially resolves. Projects onto the target-class vocabulary but names the capsule-side locality (for example, a managed_workspace target whose capsule is materialised in the managed control plane). capsule_location_class MUST be consistent with target_identity.target_class per the pairing invariants in docs/runtime/environment_capsule_contract.md.

capsule_origin_class.description: How this capsule came to exist. Every capsule names exactly one origin class; 'why this environment?' explainers resolve this field rather than inventing per-surface prose.

declarative_input_kind.description: Closed vocabulary of the declarative-input kinds a capsule may cite. Each entry names the reference kind; raw file bodies never cross this boundary.

input_ref.description: Opaque reference to the declarative input (a workspace-identity path, a template id, a flake ref, etc.). Raw file bodies are not inlined.

content_digest.description: Content digest of the declarative input at capsule-assembly time.

origin_filesystem_identity.description: Filesystem-identity record for the input when the input is a workspace file. Null for non-file inputs (managed control-plane overlays, hosted replay capsules, etc.).

secret_class.description: Re-export of the ADR-0007 secret-class vocabulary.

secret_projection_mode.description: How a secret enters the capsule. alias_only carries a credential_alias handle but no class badge; alias_plus_class_label carries both; redacted_placeholder carries a typed 'a <class> secret is present but not projected' marker; not_projected carries neither alias nor marker (the capsule declares the secret was not bound at resolve time); broadened_capture records that an explicit approval ticket authorised raw-value capture on the export path.

credential_alias_count.description: Number of credential-alias handles bound for this secret class. Raw secret values are never included.

alias_policy_ref.description: Opaque ref into the credential-broker policy rule that minted these aliases. Null when the secret is not projected.

raw_value_present.description: Always false unless the enclosing export manifest declares redaction_class broadened_capture with a recorded approval_ticket_ref.

broadened_capture_approval_ticket_ref.description: ADR-0007 approval ticket ref when projection_mode is broadened_capture. Null otherwise.

injected_delta_class.description: Env-var delta class label this secret projection produced, when applicable. Re-export of the execution-context env_var_delta_class vocabulary; raw env names and values are not inlined.

secret_projection_marker.description: One secret-class projection marker. A capsule projects N markers for N secret classes. Raw secret bytes never cross this boundary; only class labels, counts, and alias refs project. A marker with projection_mode=not_projected documents that the secret class was considered and deliberately left unbound.

policy_kind.description: Closed vocabulary of policy-classes a capsule may cite. Every marker names one kind.

policy_ref.description: Opaque policy-rule reference. Raw policy bodies never cross this boundary.

decision.description: How the policy rule landed on this capsule. broadened_with_approval MUST carry an approval_ticket_ref.

approval_ticket_ref.description: ADR-0007 approval ticket ref when decision is broadened_with_approval. Null otherwise.

capsule_policy_marker.description: One policy-bearing marker. Lets the capsule record that it sits under a specific trust gate, sandbox profile, egress posture, activator gate, or export-redaction rule without inlining the rule body.

export_sink_class.description: Closed list of export sinks a capsule body may project into. Every export path names exactly one sink.

export_redaction_class.description: Re-export of the ADR-0007 redaction-class vocabulary. metadata_and_hashes_only is the default for every export sink; broadened_capture requires an approval_ticket_ref.

field_path.description: Dotted JSON path into the capsule body (for example, 'toolchain_identity.executable_identity.presentation_path' or 'secret_projection_markers[ai_provider_token]').

admission.description: Per-field admission for this export. emitted_as_is is only admissible on fields whose value is already a class label, opaque id, hash, count, or ADR-0006 filesystem-identity record under the sink's default redaction class; raw env bodies, raw command lines, and raw secret values use emitted_as_alias_only or redacted_placeholder.

approval_ticket_ref.description: ADR-0007 approval ticket ref when redaction_class is broadened_capture. Null otherwise.

export_path_entry.description: One declared export path for this capsule. Projects the capsule body into a named sink under a named redaction class with per-field admissions. An export path is a declarative seed, not a sink implementation; sinks resolve field admissions against their own redaction defaults at emission time and MUST refuse a request that would widen admission.

capsule_degraded_reason.description: Re-export of the frozen degraded-field reason vocabulary from the execution-context schema plus the four capsule-specific reasons (declarative_input_missing, declarative_input_content_digest_mismatch, secret_projection_denied, export_path_unavailable).

authority_envelope_tag.description: Re-export of the frozen per-field authority-envelope tag vocabulary from ADR-0005 / schemas/runtime/execution_context.schema.json.

stale_reason.description: Re-export of the ADR-0005 stale-reason vocabulary. Required only when tag is 'stale' or freshness_hint indicates staleness.

consuming_surface_class.description: Surface classes that may consume this capsule. Re-exports the frozen surface_class vocabulary from schemas/runtime/execution_context.schema.json and extends it with the refactor_preview and hosted_review_replay seats this contract binds. A capsule that cannot be read by any of these surfaces is non-conforming.

capsule_hash.description: Content-addressed digest of the canonical capsule body bytes. MUST match compatibility_fingerprint.capsule_hash.

resolved_schema_version.description: Capsule-envelope schema version at resolve time. Kept as a string for forward compatibility with capsule-author-declared schemas.

declarative_inputs.description: Ordered declarative-input ledger. Every input is cited by reference and content digest; raw file bodies are not inlined.

secret_projection_markers.description: Closed set of per-secret-class projection markers. Empty is admissible and means no secret class was considered at capsule-resolve time.

policy_markers.description: Closed set of per-policy-kind markers. Empty is admissible only for capsules whose trust_state is trusted, identity_mode is account_free_local, and declarative_inputs carry no policy_overlay_ref.

declared_export_paths.description: Declared export-path seeds. Every capsule MUST declare at least one export path so later support, evidence, replay, and mutation-journal flows cite one admission table instead of improvising redaction at emission time.

consuming_surfaces.description: Closed list of surface classes that may read this capsule. Every capsule MUST bind to at least one surface; a capsule with no consumer is non-conforming.

authority_envelope.description: Per-field authority-envelope tags. Every field a consumer renders SHOULD have a tag.

degraded_fields.description: Empty means fully resolved; non-empty forces every surface that reads this capsule to render a visible honesty marker.

environment_capsule_record.description: The full environment-capsule body. Every Aureline execution surface that needs to say 'this is the environment I ran against' reads this record. The record carries target identity, toolchain identity, location class, trust state, policy epoch, declarative inputs, secret-projection markers, policy markers, and declared export paths.

environment_capsule_export_manifest_reco.description: One realised export manifest. The capsule's declared_export_paths are the declarative seeds; a manifest is the record a sink emits when it actually projects the capsule into its artefact. A manifest MUST match one of the capsule's declared_export_paths by sink_class and MUST NOT widen field_admissions beyond what the declared path authorised.

capsule_audit_event_id.description: Closed vocabulary of capsule-lifecycle audit events. Events never carry raw env bodies, raw command lines, or raw secret material.

notes_ref.description: Opaque ref into structured notes. MUST NOT embed raw env bodies, raw command lines, or raw secret material.

environment_capsule_audit_event_record.description: One capsule-lifecycle audit event. Emitted when a capsule is resolved, reused, rejected for drift, degraded, exported, its export is broadened with an approval ticket, or its schema version is bumped.
